rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY DEWITT VS. CRAZY PROTOCOL COMMUNICATIONS, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT IAC/INTERACTIVECORP'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion is denied. The purpose of CCP 474 is to avoid the statute of limitations and where a claim is not time barred, the genuine ignorance issue is irrelevant. See Davis v. Marin (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 380, 387. In this case, plaintiff seeks to recover actual and liquidated damages based on defendant's conduct. (Complaint, 6:14-18; 10:7-11). In Hypertouch v. Valueclick (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, the court explains that a 1-year statute of limitations applies to a claim for liquidated damages and a 3-year limitation applies to a claim for actual damages. Defendant fails to show that plaintiff's entire claim is time barred. Even if a portion of plaintiff's claim were untimely, it would be improper to grant this motion to quash and dispose of plaintiff's entire claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SIRIAS VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Deposition Fee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 27, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Set Deposition Fee. Pro Tem Judge Naomi Gray, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Having read and considered the motion, including the memorandum of points and authorities, all timely submitted admissible evidence, IT IS ORDERED THAT The Regents' unopposed motion to set the deposition fee of Dr. Jamasbi to the rate of $312.50 per hour is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ngray@harveysiskind.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK CHU VS. ISRAEL GONZALEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffss Motion To Vacate Judgment [C.C.P. 473]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FRANK CHU'S Motion To Vacate Judgment [C.C.P. 473]. Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Judgment is GRANTED. The general underlying purpose of Section 473(b) is to promote the determination of actions on their merits and avoid precipitating more litigation in the form of malpractice suits. (Even Zohar Const. & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 838-39.) "[C]alendar errors by an attorney or a member of his staff are, under appropriate circumstances, excusable." (Nilsson v. City of Los Angeles (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 976, 980.) Furthermore, "California courts are divided as to whether the mandatory relief provision of Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(b) applies in a context other than default." (Hossain v. Hossain (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 454, 457.) This Court holds that Plaintiff is entitled to discretionary relief under Section 473(b). Plaintiff's counsel accepts fault and responsibility for the entry of judgment in this case. There is no showing that plaintiffs bore any responsibility nor is there a showing that defendants would suffer prejudice if this matter proceeded to trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL ALEXANDER SHKLOVSKY VS. CITIBANK AKA CITIGROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SHKLOVSKY'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The motion is denied. Defendant's motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324. Plaintiff's supporting declaration fails to explain why the proposed amendments are being raised at this time. The motion is untimely and the proposed amendments change the theory/tenor of the case. Plaintiff has unreasonably delayed seeking amendment and allowing the amendment at this point of the litigation would prejudice defendant. See Fisher v. Larsen (1982) 138 Cal.App.3d 627, 649. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: K BRIAN ROMANESCHI VS. SASIMU, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Simhayoff-Cohens Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SARIT SIMHAYOFF-COHEN'S Motion To Strike. Defendant's Motion to Strike is DENIED. In ruling on a motion to strike, the allegations of a complaint are considered as a whole and are presumed to be true. (Clauson v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1253, 1255.) In order to survive a motion to strike an allegation of punitive damages, the ultimate facts showing an entitlement to such relief must be pled by a plaintiff. (Grieves v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166.) Plaintiff alleges that Defendants argreed that Plaintiff's $48,150 deposit was refundable. The facts that Plaintiff alleges in support of his claim for negligent misrepresentation support Plaintiff's claims for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: K BRIAN ROMANESCHI VS. SASIMU, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SARIT SIMHAYOFF-COHEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is OVERRULED. In ruling on a demurrer, the court considers only the material allegations pleaded in the complaint and matters subject to judicial notice. (Code Civ. Pro. § 430.30.) Based on the material allegations pleaded in Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint, Plaintiff does state facts sufficient to constitute each cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, negligent misrepresentation, conversion, breach of contract, and violation of the Consumers Legal Remedies Act (Civ. Code § 1750 et seq.)
